metadata.description: JobOS.ai first-iteration: canonical Managerial + Agent job hierarchies, T-3 DSL, context graph machine, SAP pilot.

stages.name: Research
stages.description: Survey JTBD, org design, OKR/KPI, APQC taxonomies. Propose finite Managerial catalog with counts. Propose fractal Agent hierarchy schema with 10+ worked examples.

stages.name: Synthesis
stages.description: Merge research outputs into structured handoff schema. Resolve open questions. Finalize crosswalk with translation examples.

stages.name: Decision
stages.description: Human review of open_questions requiring decisions (catalog finiteness, T-3 DSL edge cases, ethics flags). Time-boxed 48h per question.

stages.name: Engineering
stages.description: Implement: T-3 DSL engine, JSON schemas, evaluation harness, APIs (hierarchy browse, agent consumption, 2D placement), SAP pilot thin-slice.

stages.name: Evaluation
stages.description: Run evaluation harness: coverage tests, orthogonality, fractal consistency, T-3 grammaticality. Publish scoring report. Merge only when thresholds met.

t1.name: Achieve Market Position
t1.definition: Secure and grow the organization's strategic position in target markets

t1.name: Build Organizational Capability
t1.definition: Develop the internal resources, skills, and systems needed to deliver outcomes

t1.name: Ensure Operational Continuity
t1.definition: Maintain reliable, compliant, and risk-managed operations

t2.name: Acquire Customers
t2.definition: Identify, attract, and convert target buyers into paying customers

t2.name: Retain Customers
t2.definition: Maintain customer engagement and prevent churn

t2.name: Deliver Product/Service
t2.definition: Fulfill customer commitments reliably and on time

t2.name: Manage Workforce
t2.definition: Hire, develop, and retain the right people for required roles

t2.name: Manage Financial Resources
t2.definition: Allocate, track, and optimize capital and operating spend

t2.name: Manage Risk & Compliance
t2.definition: Identify, assess, and mitigate operational, legal, and reputational risk

managerial_catalog.note: T-3 catalog requires research phase output (Gemini). Placeholder.

t3_dsl_examples: Minimize the time to qualify a lead from first contact to CRM stage updated, measured in hours, target ≤ 4
Minimize the likelihood of invoice processing error due to PO mismatch, measured as error_rate in percent, target ≤ 0.5
Minimize the time to onboard a new user from account created to first feature used, measured in calendar_days, target ≤ 7
Minimize the likelihood of a qualified lead going uncontacted for > 1 business day, measured as miss_rate in percent, target ≤ 2
Minimize the time to provision all required software licenses from IT ticket creation to first login confirmed, measured in hours, target ≤ 4
Minimize the likelihood of SLA breach on P1 tickets, measured as breach_rate in per_1000, target ≤ 5
Minimize the time to complete financial close reconciliation from period end to CFO sign-off, measured in business_days, target ≤ 3
Minimize the likelihood of inventory count discrepancy detected at cycle audit, measured as defect_rate in percent, target ≤ 0.5

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Manage Financial Resources → Invoice Processing Automation

evidence.title: JobOS.ai Canonical Schemas v1.0
evidence.notes: JSON Schema for managerial_hierarchy, agent_hierarchy, crosswalk. In src/jobos/schemas/.

evidence.title: T-3 DSL Engine
evidence.notes: Pattern A/B parser, validator, renderer, translator. 39 tests passing. In src/jobos/kernel/t3_dsl.py.

open_questions.prompt: How many T-1 categories belong in the finite Managerial catalog? Current draft has 3. Literature (APQC PCF) suggests 12 process categories — should we align to APQC or define our own minimum set?
What is the correct finite count for T-2 Core Functional jobs in the Managerial catalog? Current draft has 6. Should each T-1 have exactly 3–7 T-2 children?

Should the T-3 DSL support a Pattern C for resource/cost minimization? (e.g., 'Minimize the cost of [process] from [start] to [end], measured in [currency_unit], target ≤ [N]'). This would cover financial jobs that are neither time nor likelihood.

For the SAP pilot: which SAP module and process should be the thin-slice? Candidates: (a) FI-AP invoice processing, (b) SD order-to-cash, (c) MM procurement-to-pay. Which has the clearest T-3 DSL mapping and best available public data?

Ethics/bias flag: When the Managerial catalog classifies jobs as 'AI-automatable', how do we ensure the catalog itself does not systematically undervalue human-only work categories (e.g., care work, creative work, relational work)?

acceptance_criteria.text: Managerial catalog proposes T-1, T-2, T-3 counts with definitions and 2D placement for each entry
Agent hierarchy schema includes 10+ worked T-3 DSL examples across ≥ 3 industries
All T-3 DSL examples parse and validate against the T-3 DSL engine without errors
Crosswalk covers ≥ 80% of Managerial T-2 nodes with agent counterparts
T-3 DSL parser handles Pattern A and Pattern B with ≤, <=, and < operators
JSON schemas define all tier properties, provenance, and versioning fields
API endpoints for T-3 validate, parse, render, translate, and 2D classify
Evaluation harness runs: coverage, orthogonality, fractal consistency, T-3 grammaticality. All ≥ 95% pass rate

engineering_tasks.title: Agent hierarchy JSON Schema with fractal structure
Crosswalk JSON Schema with 12 worked examples
Schema API endpoints (validate, parse, render, translate, classify-2d)
Evaluation harness (68 tests for DSL + schemas)
SAP pilot thin-slice: FI-AP ingest → hierarchy → T-3 → RAG Q&A
Pretraining data pipeline: dedup + PII filter + domain balance

tests.name: dsl_grammar_check
tests.description: All T-3 DSL examples in spec parse without error

tests.name: crosswalk_roundtrip
tests.description: Every managerial T-2 has at least one agent DSL counterpart or explicit no_equivalent

tests.name: orthogonality_check
tests.description: No T-3 statement contains technology/product names (solution-agnostic)

tests.name: fractal_consistency
tests.description: Agent hierarchy nodes at depth 1-5 all parse to valid T-3 constraints

tests.name: schema_unit_tests
tests.description: All 68 schema + DSL unit tests pass

tests.name: t3_coverage
tests.description: Worked examples cover all TimeUnit and RateType enum values
